entities.name: Proprietary acquisition channel
entities.context: A unique data sourcing mechanism that creates competitive advantage for data companies

entities.name: Data supremacy
entities.context: Unique advantage in data assets that cannot be replicated by competitors

entities.name: Structural moat
entities.context: A proprietary data source that creates irreplicable barriers to competition

entities.name: Data-first models
entities.context: Business models where the product itself generates unique, non-public data

entities.name: LinkedIn graph
entities.context: Example of a public/semi-public dataset that is commoditized and offers no sustainable advantage

entities.name: LinkedIn
entities.context: Social network whose data graph is cited as an example of commoditized public data

entities.name: Perfect competition
entities.context: Economic state where barriers are only capital and compute, leading to margin erosion

entities.name: Commodity arbitrage
entities.context: Low-value strategy of repackaging public datasets where value accrues to the source

relationships.context: Proprietary channels create structural moats by establishing irreplicable data entry points
Data-first models generate proprietary channels through unique behavioral signals and private transactions
Repackaging LinkedIn data exemplifies commoditized race-to-bottom dynamics
Public datasets lead to perfect competition where only capital and compute differentiate players
Data supremacy is achieved through structural moats that prevent replication
